title: VENDOR-PORTAL
scope: Net-new vendor-side portal. Phase 49/54 shipped Vendor (standalone model, no User row, TenantScope by landlord_id) + landlord-side assignment (Ticket.vendor_id via VendorAssignmentService, cross-tenant guarded, fires TicketAssignedToVendor + logs TicketActivity) + a one-shot signed-URL profile-completion surface (VendorProfileController under `signed`+throttle OUTSIDE the auth group; the signed URL IS the auth) + VendorObserver welcome email. Scout (2026-05-21) confirmed: there is NO vendor session/portal auth, NO acceptance flow (vendor_id is set silently), NO vendor time logging, NO vendor-facing statement or SLA view. Stripe Connect (Phase 41/42) is LANDLORD settlement (rent), not vendor payouts — so 'payout statement' is a read-only statement of vendor-attributed ticket_costs (category='vendor') + expenses, not money movement. Ticket has status (TicketStatus open/acknowledged/in_progress/resolved/closed/cancelled), vendor_id, resolution_due_at, resolved_at, costs() (ticket_costs.category in parts|vendor|labor|other), activities() (TicketActivity). Phase 70 builds the portal: VENDOR-AUTH (signed magic-link -> vendor session -> EnsureVendorPortal middleware) + TICKET-INBOX (assigned tickets + accept/decline) + JOB-ACTIONS (log time + mark resolved) + PAYOUT-STATEMENT (vendor cost statement + CSV) + SLA-DASHBOARD (vendor SLA performance) + CI. Isolation is the security spine: every query filters by the SESSION vendor id, never a client-supplied id. Six categories x 3 = 18 findings (5H/9M/4L).

categories.VENDOR-AUTH: Signed magic-link establishes a vendor-portal session; EnsureVendorPortal middleware guards /v/portal/* and re-checks the vendor is active each request. No User row — the session IS the portal identity.
categories.TICKET-INBOX: Vendor sees only tickets assigned to them (session vendor_id) and can accept or decline the assignment.
categories.JOB-ACTIONS: On an accepted ticket the vendor logs time and marks the job resolved (with resolution notes).
categories.PAYOUT-STATEMENT: Read-only statement of vendor-attributed ticket_costs + expenses over a period, with CSV export.
categories.SLA-DASHBOARD: The vendor's own SLA performance (resolution-within-SLA %, breaches, averages) from their tickets' timestamps.
categories.CI: Cross-category surface map + per-category isolation tests + runbook/memory closeout.

findings.title: Signed magic-link -> vendor-portal session + EnsureVendorPortal middleware
findings.evidence: The Phase-54 surface is one-shot per-request signed URLs for a single form; a multi-page portal (inbox, statement, SLA) needs a persistent vendor identity. There is no vendor auth/session today.
findings.remediation: GET /v/portal/enter/{vendor} (signed, 7-day, throttle:invitation, OUTSIDE the auth group) verifies the signature, sets session(['vendor_portal_id' => vendor->id]), and redirects to the portal dashboard. App\Http\Middleware\EnsureVendorPortal guards a /v/portal group: resolves Vendor::withoutGlobalScopes()->active()->find(session('vendor_portal_id')); aborts 403 (link-expired page) when absent/inactive — re-checking is_active EACH request so a deactivated vendor loses access immediately. The resolved vendor is stashed on the request (request->attributes 'portal_vendor') so controllers NEVER read a vendor id from the route/client. VendorObserver welcome email + a logout route (clears the session key). Phase70VendorAuthTest asserts: valid signed link -> session set; tampered/expired link -> 403; deactivated vendor -> 403; middleware-guarded route -> redirect/403 without a session.
findings.verification: EnsureVendorPortal + signed enter (regenerate, is_active re-check each request); request->attributes('portal_vendor'). 87e1ce5.

findings.title: Vendor portal layout + dashboard + logout
findings.evidence: The portal needs a standalone shell (vendors are not Users, so AuthenticatedLayout is wrong) and a landing page.
findings.remediation: resources/js/Layouts/VendorPortalLayout.vue (standalone — brand header, vendor name, nav to Inbox/Statement/SLA, logout) and Pages/VendorPortal/Dashboard.vue (counts: pending assignments, open jobs, SLA snapshot). VendorPortalController@dashboard reads request->attributes('portal_vendor'). POST /v/portal/logout clears the session. lang/en/vendor_portal.php ({curly}, sw [TODO-sw], ar [TODO-ar]). Phase70VendorPortalSurfaceTest asserts the layout + dashboard tokens.

findings.title: Landlord re-issue portal link + artisan command
findings.evidence: Signed links expire (7 days); the landlord/operator needs a way to re-send without recreating the vendor.
findings.remediation: A landlord action POST /vendors/{vendor}/portal-link (VendorPolicy-gated, owning landlord) re-queues the welcome email with a fresh signed portal link; a `vendor:portal-link {vendor}` artisan command prints/queues one for operator use. Both reuse a single VendorPortalLinkService::issue(Vendor): string (URL::signedRoute, 7-day) so the expiry/route is defined once. Phase70VendorAuthTest asserts the service mints a verifiable signed URL + the landlord route is owner-gated (cross-tenant 403).

findings.title: Vendor assignment acceptance state
findings.evidence: VendorAssignmentService sets ticket.vendor_id silently — the vendor never accepts/declines, so the landlord can't tell if the contractor took the job.
findings.remediation: Migration adds tickets.vendor_status enum(pending,accepted,declined) nullable + vendor_responded_at nullable. VendorAssignmentService::assign sets vendor_status=pending + vendor_responded_at=null on (re)assignment. Ticket gains helpers vendorPending()/vendorAccepted(). A VendorAssignmentResponseService::accept(Ticket)/decline(Ticket,reason) stamps the status + responded_at, logs TicketActivity, and on decline clears vendor_id (back to the landlord pool) + fires an event the landlord is notified on. Phase70TicketInboxTest asserts accept/decline transitions + decline clears vendor_id + cross-vendor cannot respond.
findings.verification: tickets.vendor_status/vendor_responded_at (migration in VENDOR-AUTH as foundation) + VendorAssignmentResponseService accept/decline (lockForUpdate race guard; decline clears vendor_id + VendorDeclinedAssignment->NotifyLandlordOnVendorDecline). 156fdca.

findings.title: Vendor-scoped inbox + accept/decline endpoints
findings.evidence: The vendor needs to see ONLY their assigned tickets and act on them — the #1 IDOR risk in the portal.
findings.remediation: VendorPortalTicketController@index lists Ticket::where('vendor_id', portal_vendor->id) (the session vendor, NEVER a client id) with status + SLA due + property/unit context; @accept / @decline POST routes resolve the ticket then assert ticket.vendor_id === portal_vendor->id before delegating to VendorAssignmentResponseService (a second vendor cannot accept another's ticket even with a guessed id -> 403/404). All under the EnsureVendorPortal group + throttle. Phase70TicketInboxTest asserts a vendor sees only their tickets, a cross-vendor ticket id 403/404s, and accept/decline require ownership.
findings.verification: VendorPortalTicketController index/accept/decline, session-vendor scoped + ownership 403 + pending/open 422. 156fdca.

findings.title: Inbox Vue page + accept/decline UI
findings.evidence: No UI to view/accept/decline assigned jobs.
findings.remediation: Pages/VendorPortal/Inbox.vue: pending vs active sections, accept/decline buttons (decline opens a reason field), SLA-due badge, link to the job page. data-testid tokens (vendor-ticket-row, vendor-accept, vendor-decline). lang vendor_portal.inbox.* ({curly}). Phase70VendorPortalSurfaceTest asserts the page + tokens.

findings.title: Vendor time logging
findings.evidence: There is no record of vendor labour time; it underpins both the statement and SLA views.
findings.remediation: Migration creates ticket_time_logs (id, ticket_id, vendor_id, minutes unsigned, note nullable, logged_at, timestamps; index ticket_id+vendor_id). TicketTimeLog model. VendorTimeLogService::log(Ticket, Vendor, minutes, note) guards ticket.vendor_id === vendor->id AND vendor_status=accepted (can't log on a job you haven't accepted), validates minutes 1..1440. POST /v/portal/tickets/{ticket}/time under EnsureVendorPortal, ownership re-checked. Phase70JobActionsTest asserts a log row is written, a non-owned ticket is rejected, and logging on a non-accepted ticket is rejected.
findings.verification: ticket_time_logs + VendorTimeLogService (ownership+accepted gate, lockForUpdate) + logTime endpoint (minutes 1..1440, throttle:10,1). 16c0205.

findings.title: Vendor mark-resolved
findings.evidence: A vendor who finishes a job has no way to signal completion.
findings.remediation: VendorJobResolutionService::resolve(Ticket, Vendor, notes) guards ownership + vendor_status=accepted + ticket is open-ish, sets status=Resolved + resolved_at + resolution_notes, records the vendor as resolver in TicketActivity (so the landlord sees who/when), fires the existing resolution path/event so the landlord can close. POST /v/portal/tickets/{ticket}/resolve. Phase70JobActionsTest asserts resolve transitions status + records the vendor + ownership-gated.
findings.verification: VendorJobResolutionService resolve (lock + accepted + isOpen; records vendor_resolved activity). 16c0205.

findings.title: Job detail Vue page (time + resolve)
findings.evidence: No UI to log time or mark resolved.
findings.remediation: Pages/VendorPortal/Job.vue: ticket summary, time-log form (minutes + note) with a running total, mark-resolved form (resolution notes), prior time logs list. data-testid (vendor-time-form, vendor-resolve-form). lang vendor_portal.job.*. Phase70VendorPortalSurfaceTest asserts page + tokens + route /v/portal/tickets/{ticket}.
findings.verification: Job.vue (time form + resolve form + entries, accepted-only); show() filters time logs by session vendor (no cross-vendor leak). 16c0205.

findings.title: Vendor cost statement service + view
findings.evidence: A vendor cannot see what work/cost has been recorded against them. Stripe Connect is landlord-settlement, not vendor payout — this is a read-only statement.
findings.remediation: VendorStatementService::forVendor(Vendor, from, to): aggregates ticket_costs WHERE category='vendor' on tickets assigned to the vendor + Expense rows linked to the vendor, grouped by ticket + month, with totals — all landlord+vendor scoped (no cross-tenant rows). VendorPortalStatementController@index (period filter, default last 90d). Read-only; no money movement. Phase70StatementTest asserts the statement totals only the vendor's own attributed costs and excludes another vendor's / another landlord's.
findings.verification: VendorStatementService (vendor-category ticket_costs + expenses, scoped, cents) + statement endpoint. Read-only (Stripe Connect is landlord settlement). 9ef1b3c.

findings.title: Statement CSV export
findings.evidence: Vendors need the statement as a document for their own books.
findings.remediation: VendorPortalStatementController@export streams a UTF-8 BOM CSV (reusing the Phase-65 csvEscape injection guard: neutralise leading =,+,-,@) of the period's line items, scoped to the session vendor. Streamed response (download), session-vendor only. Phase70StatementTest asserts the CSV contains the vendor's rows + injection neutralisation + cross-vendor exclusion.
findings.verification: toCsv UTF-8 BOM + injection guard; streamed export, session-scoped. 9ef1b3c.

findings.title: Statement Vue page + period filter
findings.remediation: Pages/VendorPortal/Statement.vue: period selector, grouped line items (per ticket/month), total in KES en-KE via useFormatters, CSV download button. data-testid (vendor-statement, vendor-statement-export). lang vendor_portal.statement.*. Phase70VendorPortalSurfaceTest asserts page + tokens.
findings.verification: Statement.vue period filter + grouped line items + KES totals + CSV. 9ef1b3c.

findings.title: Vendor SLA performance service
findings.evidence: Vendors have no visibility into their own response/resolution performance; landlords have SLA gauges but vendors don't.
findings.remediation: VendorSlaService::forVendor(Vendor, windowDays=90): over the vendor's resolved tickets, computes resolution-within-SLA % (resolved_at <= resolution_due_at), breach count, total resolved, and average resolution time, plus open-overdue count (breachedResolutionSla scope filtered to the vendor). All vendor-scoped. Phase70SlaDashboardTest asserts the math on a seeded mix of within-SLA / breached / open-overdue tickets, vendor-scoped.

findings.title: Vendor SLA dashboard endpoint
findings.evidence: The metrics need a portal surface.
findings.remediation: VendorPortalSlaController@index returns the VendorSlaService payload for the session vendor (window selectable). EnsureVendorPortal-guarded; session vendor only. Phase70SlaDashboardTest asserts the endpoint returns the vendor's metrics + is unreachable without a portal session.

findings.title: SLA dashboard Vue page
findings.evidence: No UI for the SLA metrics.
findings.remediation: Pages/VendorPortal/Sla.vue: within-SLA % ring, breach + open-overdue counts, average resolution time, window selector. data-testid (vendor-sla, vendor-sla-within). lang vendor_portal.sla.*. Phase70VendorPortalSurfaceTest asserts page + tokens.
findings.verification: Sla.vue within-SLA ring + cards + window selector. 6aab57a.

findings.title: Phase70VendorPortalSurfaceTest cross-category map
findings.evidence: The portal surface (middleware, routes, columns, table, services, Vue, lang) must be guarded against drift.
findings.remediation: tests/Feature/VendorPortal/Phase70VendorPortalSurfaceTest asserts: EnsureVendorPortal middleware + VendorPortal* controllers + VendorStatementService/VendorSlaService/VendorTimeLogService/VendorAssignmentResponseService/VendorPortalLinkService classes exist; tickets.vendor_status + vendor_responded_at columns + ticket_time_logs table present; routes (v/portal/enter, dashboard, inbox, ticket accept/decline/time/resolve, statement(+export), sla, logout) registered; Vue (VendorPortalLayout + Dashboard/Inbox/Job/Statement/Sla) exist; vendor_portal lang loads.
findings.verification: Phase70VendorPortalSurfaceTest (6 methods) maps classes/columns/routes/Vue/command/lang + portal session-guard.

findings.title: Per-category isolation feature tests
findings.evidence: The portal's whole risk is cross-vendor / cross-tenant data access; behaviour must be asserted, not just presence.
findings.remediation: Phase70VendorAuthTest (signed link -> session, expired/tampered/deactivated -> 403, middleware gate). Phase70TicketInboxTest (vendor sees only own tickets, cross-vendor 403/404, accept/decline + decline clears vendor_id). Phase70JobActionsTest (time log + resolve, ownership + accepted gate). Phase70StatementTest (own costs only, CSV injection guard, cross-vendor exclusion). Phase70SlaDashboardTest (SLA math, vendor-scoped). All assert the SESSION vendor is authoritative, never a client id.

findings.title: Runbook + memory closeout
findings.evidence: Operators + the cycle ledger need the portal documented.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/vendor-portal.md (auth model, link re-issue, isolation contract, statement/SLA semantics) + maintenance.md cross-link. Memory: project_propmanager_phase70_plan + pending-path + forward-path + MEMORY.md updated; audit_closeout merged into phase-70-audit-prd.json; CodeRabbit run recorded per logic-bearing sub-phase diff.
findings.verification: docs/runbooks/vendor-portal.md; this closeout; memory + CodeRabbit per logic-bearing diff.

audit_closeout.result: 18/18 findings closed (zero deferrals). Net-new vendor portal.
audit_closeout.tests: 38 VendorPortal tests / 189 assertions green (6 files); 107 Maintenance+Vendors regression green; build + import gate green; Pint clean.
deviations: PAYOUT-STATEMENT is a read-only cost statement, not a money movement — Stripe Connect (Phase 41/42) is landlord settlement (rent), there is no vendor disbursement rail. Documented in the PRD + runbook.
The tickets.vendor_status + vendor_responded_at migration landed in VENDOR-AUTH (not TICKET-INBOX) because the portal dashboard's pending-count needs it as a data foundation.
coderabbit: VENDOR-AUTH: session->regenerate() on enter (anti-fixation) + reuse breachedResolutionSla scope (low).

TICKET-INBOX: HIGH — concurrent accept/decline race -> lockForUpdate + re-assert pending; terminal-status guard (can't respond to a closed ticket); capture declining vendor id in the audit before nulling.
JOB-ACTIONS: HIGH — resolve()/log() re-assert under lockForUpdate (reassign race); show() filters time logs by session vendor (cross-vendor leak on reassignment); throttle:10,1 on time route.
